name: Climb to the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Meadow (Viver de la Sierra, Sestrica) from El Frasno
name_original: Subida a la Ermita de Nuestra Señora del Prado (Viver de la Sierra, Sestrica) desde El Frasno
region_intro: Road cycling around El Frasno, situated at the foot of the Sierra de Vicor and bordering the Sierra de Algairén, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features an extensive network of roads, often quiet, traversing varied topography with both gentle slopes and challenging mountain passes. Cyclists can expect ascents that lead to expansive views, with landscapes characterized by pine, holm oak, and cork oak forests. The area's natural features include significant elevations like Pico del Rayo and mountain passes such as Collado de la Luz. Best road cycling routes around El Frasno The most popular road cycling route is Climb to the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Meadow (Viver de la Sierra, Sestrica) from El Frasno, a 27.0 miles (43.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a notable climb to a hermitage, offering elevated perspectives. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Calatayud – ️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Morata de Jalón, a difficult 73.0 miles (117.5 km) path. This extensive loop passes by the landmark Puerta de Terrer, providing a challenging ride through varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Peña de la Mora – Puerto de Embid loop from El Frasno, a 29.3 miles (47.2 km) trail leading through Peña de la Mora and Puerto de Embid, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around El Frasno is defined by the Sierra de Vicor and Sierra de Algairén mountain ranges, diverse forests, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in El Frasno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 6 reviews. More than 450 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Frasno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around El Frasno?
A: The region around El Frasno, nestled between the Sierra de Vicor and Sierra de Algairén, offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find an extensive network of roads, often quiet, with both gentle slopes and challenging mountain passes. Expect significant ascents that lead to expansive views, traversing landscapes characterized by pine, holm oak, and cork oak forests.

Q: Are there routes suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, road cycling around El Frasno caters to various experience levels. The area features routes ranging from moderate to difficult, with options for those seeking challenging mountain passes and others preferring less strenuous climbs. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990765790/climb-to-the-hermitage-of-our-lady-of-the-meadow-viver-de-la-sierra-sestrica-from-el-frasno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Meadow&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route, while &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud – ⭐️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Morata de Jalón&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while cycling?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can cycle past majestic peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2612188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Rayo&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point of the Sierra de Vicor, offering magnificent views. Other notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Luz&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5259458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Espino Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capurnos Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the El Frasno area?
A: There are 16 road cycling routes available around El Frasno, offering a variety of distances and challenges. These include 6 moderate routes and 10 difficult routes, ensuring options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40338707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Mora – Puerto de Embid loop from El Frasno&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 47.2 km ride.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in El Frasno?
A: The road cycling routes in El Frasno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 6 reviews. Over 450 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the quiet roads and the stunning views from the mountain passes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in El Frasno?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's diverse landscapes and mountain passes suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant cycling conditions, with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures to higher elevations.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in this area?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for endurance cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud – ⭐️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Morata de Jalón&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 117.5 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20146445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;⭐️ Puerta de Terrer – 🍝 Nuévalos loop from Paracuellos-Sabiñán&lt;/a&gt; covers 105.7 km.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through towns or villages where I can stop for refreshments?
A: While specific cafes or pubs aren't detailed, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud – ⭐️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Morata de Jalón&lt;/a&gt; pass by significant settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2092543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud&lt;/a&gt;, offering opportunities to find refreshments and explore local culture.

Q: What kind of natural scenery will I encounter on the routes?
A: The routes around El Frasno are characterized by rich biodiversity and forested scenery. You'll cycle through forests of pine, holm oak, cork oak, and maple trees, which contribute to extensive panoramic views. The area is also part of a recognized Special Protection Area for birds, adding an ecological dimension to your ride.

Q: Are there any routes that offer particularly challenging climbs?
A: For cyclists seeking challenging climbs, the region's mountain ranges, Sierra de Vicor and Sierra de Algairén, provide ample opportunities. Routes often feature significant ascents to peaks and passes, such as those leading towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2612188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Rayo&lt;/a&gt; or through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Luz&lt;/a&gt;, rewarding riders with breathtaking vistas.

Q: Can I find routes that are less frequented by traffic?
A: Yes, the region around El Frasno is known for its extensive network of roads that are often quiet and have low traffic. This enhances the enjoyment of the natural surroundings and provides a more serene cycling experience away from busy thoroughfares.
tips:
We are in Viver de la Sierra belonging to the municipality of Sestrica, a very picturesque town during the spring months in which the cherry trees that surround the town bloom.
Nearby, we will find the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Prado, well rebuilt at the end of the last century and destination for road bike lovers as the final point of their ascent.
